title: crotalaria arenicola
intro: Crotalaria arenicola, also known as the coastal rattlebox, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Australia, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. Crotalaria arenicola is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections, as well as providing relief from fever and pain. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be found in some traditional medicine systems, including Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
